steps.title: Factor the radicand
steps.content:
The expression under the fourth root is a perfect square trinomial.
$$x^2+10x+25=(x+5)^2$$

steps.title: Apply the fourth root
steps.content:
Rewrite the fourth root using exponents. Because $x>0$, $x+5$ is positive.
$$\sqrt[4]{(x+5)^2}=((x+5)^2)^{1/4}$$
$$\sqrt[4]{(x+5)^2}=(x+5)^{1/2}$$
The answer is D.
